---
title: "Lecture 01: Prepare Environments"
execute:
  enabled: false
---

This note covers the required environment setup for the course.

## 1) Install Ubuntu 22.04 {.unnumbered}

Use **Ubuntu 22.04 LTS** with one of the following options:

- Replace your current OS with Ubuntu
- Dual boot (Ubuntu + your current OS)
- Run Ubuntu in a virtual machine

References:

- Ubuntu 22.04 download: [Ubuntu 22.04 LTS](https://releases.ubuntu.com/22.04/)
- Official installation guide: [Install Ubuntu Desktop](https://ubuntu.com/tutorials/install-ubuntu-desktop)
- Virtual machine option (VirtualBox): [VirtualBox Downloads](https://www.virtualbox.org/wiki/Downloads)

## 2) Create Git ID and learn Git {.unnumbered}

Create your GitHub account and configure your local Git identity.

- GitHub sign up: [Create GitHub account](https://github.com/signup)
- Git installation: [Git Downloads](https://git-scm.com/downloads)

Set your Git identity:

```bash
git config --global user.name "Your Name"
git config --global user.email "you@example.com"
```

Tutorials:

- Interactive tutorial: [Learn Git Branching](https://learngitbranching.js.org/)
- Reference book: [Pro Git Book](https://git-scm.com/book/en/v2)

## 3) Set up Conda environment {.unnumbered}

Install Miniconda, then create a course environment.

- Miniconda installation: [Miniconda docs](https://docs.conda.io/en/latest/miniconda.html)
- Conda environments guide: [Managing environments](https://docs.conda.io/projects/conda/en/latest/user-guide/tasks/manage-environments.html)

Example setup:

```bash
conda create -n rslab python=3.10 -y
conda activate rslab
```

## 4) Use VS Code {.unnumbered}

Install and use VS Code for coding, notebook editing, and Git workflow.

- VS Code installation (Linux): [VS Code setup](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs/setup/linux)
- VS Code docs: [Get started with VS Code](https://code.visualstudio.com/docs)

Recommended extensions:

- Python
- Jupyter
- GitLens
